searchDocs.title: Quick links
searchDocs.content: Quick links A fast, efficient, and secure state manager built for mass and Minute control of instances and states Installation guideAPI DocsExamplesChange Log

searchDocs.title: Method #1 - Roblox​
searchDocs.content: Download straight from the roblox marketplace this will only contain the module itself.

searchDocs.title: Method #2 - Github​
searchDocs.content: Download straight from the releases subdirectory of this repository

searchDocs.title: Download options​
searchDocs.content: Install the Ecosystem.V2.4.1WithDependencies.zip file for just the runtime module with its dependenciesInstall the Ecosystem.V2.4.1WithTests file for runtime and test scriptsInstall the Ecosystem.V2.4.1 file WITHOUT dependencies. Dependencies are needed to run

searchDocs.title: Method #3 - Git Clone​
searchDocs.content: Clone this repository straight into your project with Git

searchDocs.title: Git steps:​
searchDocs.content: git clone https://github.com/Green-Screen/Ecosystem.git

searchDocs.title: Dependencies:​
searchDocs.content: EasyTypeTramServiceSecureClassesSmartTable (No repo)

searchDocs.title: NewEcosystem​
searchDocs.content: Constructor </> EcosystemGlobalServices.NewEcosystem( Identifyer: string-- the ID assigned to the current Ecosystem object ) → Ecosystem Creates a new Ecosystem object Example local Ecosystem = require("Path to ecosystem module") local EcosystemObject1 = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("TestEcosystem") print(EcosystemObject1.ClassName) -- Ecosystem

searchDocs.title: NewSpecies​
searchDocs.content: Constructor </> EcosystemGlobalServices.NewSpecies( Identifyer: string,-- the ID assigned to the current Ecosystem object ObjectToBind: Instance? -- The optional instance to be binded to the species object ) → Species Creates a new Species object from the passed Ecosystem Identifyer Must freeze Before creating a Species object you must first call Ecosystem:Freeze() you can check if a Ecosystem is frozen with Ecosystem.Frozen Example local Ecosystem = require("Path to ecosystem module") local EcosystemObject1 = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("TestEcosystem") print(EcosystemObject1.ClassName) -- Ecosystem -- Turns the Ecosystem object into a read-only template EcosystemObject1:Freeze() local TestSpecies = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("TestEcosystem") print(TestSpecies.ClassName) -- Species

searchDocs.title: GetSpeciesFromBindedInstance​
searchDocs.content: </> EcosystemGlobalServices:GetSpeciesFromBindedInstance( I: Instance ,-- A instance that potentially has a species object binded to it Controller: Ecosystem?-- Searches the Controller object instead of global ) → Species? Gets the species object binded to a instance Time-Complexity Providing a controller object will change the time complexity to O(n) instead of the default without O(n^2). NOTE if a controller is provided it will only search the controller that is passed. Example local Ecosystem = require("Path to ecosystem module") local EcosystemObject1 = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("TestEcosystem") local TestNPC = workspace.NPC -- Turns the Ecosystem object into a read-only template EcosystemObject1:Freeze() local TestSpecies = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("TestEcosystem", TestNPC) print(TestSpecies.ObjectBinded == TestNPC) -- true -- The same Species object local TestSpecies2 = Ecosystem:GetSpeciesFromBindedInstance(TestNPC, EcosystemObject1) print(TestSpecies2.ObjectBinded == TestNPC) -- true

searchDocs.title: GetEcosystemFromSpecie​
searchDocs.content: </> EcosystemGlobalServices:GetEcosystemFromSpecie( S: Species-- The species you want to get the controller for ) → Ecosystem? Gets the Ecosystem object binded to the passed Species Example local Ecosystem = require("Path to ecosystem module") local EcosystemObject1 = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("TestEcosystem") -- Turns the Ecosystem object into a read-only template EcosystemObject1:Freeze() local TestSpecies = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("TestEcosystem") print(EcosystemObject1.EcosystemID) -- TestEcosystem -- The same Ecosystem object local EcosystemObject2 = Ecosystem:GetEcosystemFromSpecie(TestSpecies) print(EcosystemObject1.EcosystemID) -- TestEcosystem

searchDocs.title: 1. Creating an Ecosystem object​
searchDocs.content: -- Get Dependencies local Players = game:GetService("Players") -- Production state test local Ecosystem = require("../") -- Initializes an Ecosystem with the ID of "RoamingChasingNPC" local TestController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("RoamingChasingNPC") -- A function that will be used throughout the demonstration local function GetClosestPlayer(Pos: Vector3): (Player, number) local ClosestPlr, ClosestDis = nil, math.huge for _, V: Player in Players:GetPlayers() do local char = V.Character if char then if (char.HumanoidRootPart.Position - Pos).magnitude < ClosestDis then ClosestDis = (char.HumanoidRootPart.Position - Pos).magnitude ClosestPlr = V end end end print(ClosestPlr, ClosestDis) return ClosestPlr, ClosestDis end

searchDocs.title: Creating the Ecosystem Template​
searchDocs.content: --[[ Arguably the most time intensive part. This is when you create your template icluding creating the State functions and Gloabl and local properties ]] -- Creates a custom `local` property with the index of "Host" and initial value of "nil" TestController:AddCustomProperty("Host") -- Creates a custom `global` properties with a index and initial value TestController:SetFunctionEnviorment("ActivationDistance", 100) TestController:SetFunctionEnviorment("ChaseSpeed", 23) TestController:SetFunctionEnviorment("RoamSpeed", 16) --[[ Creates a new state with the ID of "Roam" and the function it should run. ALL state functions must return a valid state ID, "Stop", or call CurrentSpecies:Stop() It is recommended to use While loops instead of Run service loops becuase While loops are thread controlled while RunService events are RBXScriptSignals and run on a seperate thread. This makes them hard to control and could create some logic errors if used. ]] TestController:AddStateAndFeature("Roam", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string local Humanoid: Humanoid = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.Humanoid Humanoid.WalkSpeed = CurrentSpecies:GetEnviorment("RoamSpeed") while task.wait(0.1) do local ClosestPlr, Dis = GetClosestPlayer(CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.HumanoidRootPart.Position) if Dis < CurrentSpecies:GetEnviorment("ActivationDistance") then CurrentSpecies:SetProperty("Host", ClosestPlr) return "Chase" else Humanoid:MoveTo(Vector3.new(math.random(700, 750), 0, math.random(400, 450))) end end return "Roam" end) --[[ Creates a new state with the ID of "Chase" and the function it should run. ]] TestController:AddStateAndFeature("Chase", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string local Humanoid: Humanoid = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.Humanoid local ClosestPlr: Player = CurrentSpecies:GetProperty("Host") while task.wait() do Humanoid.WalkSpeed = CurrentSpecies:GetEnviorment("ChaseSpeed") local _, Dis = GetClosestPlayer(CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.HumanoidRootPart.Position) if Dis > CurrentSpecies:GetEnviorment("ActivationDistance") then return "Roam" else if Dis < 3 then ClosestPlr.Character.Humanoid.Health -= 10 end Humanoid:MoveTo(ClosestPlr.Character.PrimaryPart.Position) end end end) --[[ Creates a new state with the ID of "Lock" and the function it should run. This function is unable to return itself thus freezeing the Species, The only way to unlock them is by using the Species object its assigned to and call `Stop` or `ForceState` to change the state manually. You are also able to use the Ecosystem class to control a mass amount of species easily ]] TestController:AddStateAndFeature("Lock", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string while task.wait() do print("Stunned") end end)

searchDocs.title: Freezing​
searchDocs.content: --[[ The most important part of the Ecosystem object. You must call Freeze at the end to signify that the template is contructed. This will turn the object into a read-only state and will allow Species objects to be created and assigned to it's template. ]] -- Freezes the controller to allow Specie object to be created. TestController:Freeze()

searchDocs.title: 2. Creating an Species​
searchDocs.content: --[[ Once a Ecosystem template/Controller is created AND FROZEN. You are able to create Specie (runtim) objects assigned to it. ]] print(TestController.Frozen) -- true -- Reference to a NPC model local TestHost = script.Parent.Parent.Parent.TestObjects.StateTest --[[ Creates a specie object from the Ecosystem template of "RoamingChasingNPC" And links the NPC model to it ]] local SpeciesTest = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("RoamingChasingNPC", TestHost)

searchDocs.title: Running a Species object​
searchDocs.content: -- The species object created above local SpeciesTest = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("RoamingChasingNPC", TestHost) -- Starts the runtime at the state "Roam" SpeciesTest:Start("Roam")

searchDocs.title: Updating a Enviorment Variable​
searchDocs.content: task.wait(10) -- Updates the global enviorment variable "ChaseSpeed" to 80 TestController:UpdateEnviormentVariable("ChaseSpeed", 80) -- This will also trigger the `EnviormentUpdate` signal to run warn("Updated")

searchDocs.title: Forcing a state at runtime​
searchDocs.content: -- Forces the state `Lock` on the current species SpeciesTest:ForceState("Lock") task.wait(3) -- Stops the species object from running SpeciesTest:Stop() -- Reruns lock SpeciesTest:Start("Lock") task.wait(1) -- WILL ERROR as it tries to update using the Start function SpeciesTest:Start("Lock") -- Use FroceState if you want to update a state manually SpeciesTest:ForceState("Roam")

searchDocs.title: Species​
searchDocs.content: </> type Species = SecuredClass <Species> The Species object is wrapped inside of the SecuredClass this provides protections such as proper read-only properties + more

searchDocs.title: SpeciesID​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Species.SpeciesID: string The UUID that is assigned when the Species object is created

searchDocs.title: AttachedEcosystemID​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Species.AttachedEcosystemID: string The identifier string that is passes in the NewSpecies constructor that matches the Ecosystem that you want to bind it to.

searchDocs.title: ClassName​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Species.ClassName: string The class of the object Species

searchDocs.title: CurrentState​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Species.CurrentState: string The current running state ID nil if non is running

searchDocs.title: ObjectBinded​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Species.ObjectBinded: Instance?  A optional paramater that allows you to link a instance to the species object

searchDocs.title: Opertable​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Species.Opertable: true? A boolean to check if the Species object has been destroyed or not. It is permenetly read-only

searchDocs.title: StateChanged​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only Signal </> Species.StateChanged: Tram<StateFrom:string,StateTo:string)> A tram signal class that fires whenever a State is changed in the order of (StateFrom:string, StateTo:string)

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Species:Destroy( Nuke: Boolean-- If instances should be destroyed or dereferenced ) → nil Destroys the Species object instantly and sets Opertable to nil Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") local Specie1 = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("JumpingNPC", NPCModel::model) -- The specie object will be unbinded and removed from the Ecosystem Binded objects cache Specie1:Destroy() Or: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") local Specie1 = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("JumpingNPC", NPCModel::model) -- The specie object will be unbinded and removed from the Ecosystem Binded objects cache while also destroying the NPCModel Specie1:Destroy(true)

searchDocs.title: Start​
searchDocs.content: </> Species:Start( StateID: string-- must be a valid State that was assigned in the ecosystem template ) → () Starts the FSM runtime for the species called on passing the first StateID as the initial state to run. Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Jump10Times", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string local Count = 0 local CurrentObjectHumanoid:Humanoid = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.Humanoid while task.wait(0.1) do if not CurrentObjectHumanoid.Jump then CurrentObjectHumanoid.Jump = true Count += 1 end if Count >= 10 then return "NextState" -- The next StateID that should run end end end) -- Freezes the controller making it read-only NPCController:Freeze() -- Creates a Species Class from the Ecosystem template local JumpingNPC = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("JumpingNPC", NPCInstance) -- Begins cycle JumpingNPC:Start("Jump10Times")

searchDocs.title: Stop​
searchDocs.content: </> Species:Stop() → () Stops the current Species runtime instantly by closing the thread Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") local HasJumped10Times = false NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Jump10Times", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string local Count = 0 local CurrentObjectHumanoid:Humanoid = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.Humanoid while task.wait(0.1) do if not CurrentObjectHumanoid.Jump then CurrentObjectHumanoid.Jump = true Count += 1 end if Count >= 10 then HasJumped10Times = true end end end) -- Freezes the controller making it read-only NPCController:Freeze() -- Creates a Species Class from the Ecosystem template local JumpingNPC = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("JumpingNPC", NPCInstance) -- Begins cycle JumpingNPC:Start("Jump10Times") while task.wait() do if HasJumped10Times then -- Stops the running species instantly JumpingNPC:Stop() break end end Or alternatively: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Jump10Times", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string local Count = 0 local CurrentObjectHumanoid:Humanoid = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.Humanoid while task.wait(0.1) do if not CurrentObjectHumanoid.Jump then CurrentObjectHumanoid.Jump = true Count += 1 end if Count >= 10 then -- Returning "Stop" will cause the running thread to yield and gc -- This method DOES NOT guarantee instant results as it is -- dependent on the current cycle of the GC return "Stop" end end end) -- Freezes the controller making it read-only NPCController:Freeze() -- Creates a Species Class from the Ecosystem template local JumpingNPC = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("JumpingNPC", NPCInstance) -- Begins cycle JumpingNPC:Start("Jump10Times")

searchDocs.title: ForceState​
searchDocs.content: </> Species:ForceState( StateID: string-- must be a valid State that was assigned in the ecosystem template ) → () Forcefully changes the current running state even if a state is mid execution Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Jump10Times", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string -- implmentation the same as above end) NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Wander", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string -- implemtation is user definded -- Ex: -- Makes a NPC wander randomly in a 100x100 box around a fixed point end) -- Freezes the controller making it read-only NPCController:Freeze() -- Creates a Species Class from the Ecosystem template local JumpingNPC = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("JumpingNPC", NPCInstance) -- Begins cycle JumpingNPC:Start("Jump10Times") task.wait(2) -- Forces the wander state JumpingNPC:ForceState("Wander") Equal to: local JumpingNPC = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("JumpingNPC", NPCInstance) JumpingNPC:Start("Jump10Times") task.wait(2) JumpingNPC:ForceState("Wander") -- Or Simplily JumpingNPC:Start("Jump10Times") task.wait(2) -- Stops the current state JumpingNPC:Stop() -- CAUTION: Adding a wait my be necessary due to task scheduler priorities JumpingNPC:Start("Wander")

searchDocs.title: GetEnviorment​
searchDocs.content: </> Species:GetEnviorment( EnvVar: string-- The index of the enviorment variable ) → any Returns the value of a enviorment variable with the given index Read-Only The Species object is NOT able to write to EnviormentVariables, only read. Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") NPCController:SetFunctionEnviorment("GlobalVariable", 10) -- Initial value of 10 NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Jump10Times", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string print(CurrentSpecies:GetEnviorment("GlobalVariable")) -- 10 print(CurrentSpecies:GetEnviorment("GlobalVariable2")) -- nil print(CurrentSpecies:GetEnviorment()) -- The dictionary of enviorment properties return "NextState" -- Next state end)

searchDocs.title: SetProperty​
searchDocs.content: </> Species:SetProperty( Property: string,-- The index of the local property value Value: any-- the value to be assigned ) → () Updates a internal variable that is local to the species object Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") NPCController:AddCustomProperty("LocalVariable", true) -- Initial value of true NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Jump10Times", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string print(CurrentSpecies:GetProperty("LocalVariable")) -- true CurrentSpecies:SetProperty("LocalVariable", false) -- updates to false print(CurrentSpecies:GetProperty("LocalVariable")) -- false -- Properties must be apart of the template created in the `Ecosystem` object CurrentSpecies:SetProperty("LocalVariable2", "init") -- Error: Attempt to assign nil property return "NextState" -- Next state end)

searchDocs.title: GetProperty​
searchDocs.content: </> Species:GetProperty( Property: string-- The index of the local property value ) → any Returns the value of a property index or if nil the whole dictionary Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") NPCController:AddCustomProperty("LocalVariable", true) -- Initial value of true NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Jump10Times", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string print(CurrentSpecies:GetProperty("LocalVariable")) -- true CurrentSpecies:SetProperty("LocalVariable", false) -- updates to false print(CurrentSpecies:GetProperty("LocalVariable")) -- false print(CurrentSpecies:GetProperty()) -- Returns property dictionary return "NextState" -- Next state end)

searchDocs.title: Ecosystem​
searchDocs.content: </> type Ecosystem = SecuredClass <Ecosystem> The Ecosystem object is wrapped inside of the SecuredClass this provides protections such as proper read-only properties + more

searchDocs.title: FunctionState​
searchDocs.content: </> type FunctionState = ( CurrentSpecies: Species, StateFrom: string? ) → string  Return A feature function MUST return a valid StateID string for the next state to run or Stop to stop close the current running thread

searchDocs.title: EcosystemID​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Ecosystem.EcosystemID: string The identifier string that is passes in the NewEcosystem contstructor

searchDocs.title: Frozen​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Ecosystem.Frozen: Boolean A boolean that represents if the Ecosystem template is done being constructed and is ready to have Specie subclasses created from it.

searchDocs.title: Opertable​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Ecosystem.Opertable: true? A boolean to check if the Ecosystem object has been destroyed or not. It is permenetly read-only

searchDocs.title: ClassName​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only </> Ecosystem.ClassName: string The class of the object Ecosystem

searchDocs.title: EnviormentUpdate​
searchDocs.content: This item is read only and cannot be modified. Read Only Signal </> Ecosystem.EnviormentUpdate: Tram<EnviormentVariableIndex:string,NewData:any,OldData:any)> A tram signal class that fires whenever a enviorment Global variable is changed in the order of (EnviormentVariableIndex:string, NewData:any, OldData:any)

searchDocs.title: Destroy​
searchDocs.content: </> Ecosystem:Destroy( Nuke: Boolean-- If instances should be destroyed or dereferenced ) → nil Destroys the Species object instantly and sets Opertable to nil Recursive Calling destroy on a Ecosystem object will cause ALL species objects binded to the ecosystem to also be destroyed. Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") -- Destroys the ecosystem object and all binded Species objects NPCController:Destroy() Or: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") -- Destroys the ecosystem object and all binded Species objects and their instances NPCController:Destroy(true)

searchDocs.title: AddStateFeature​
searchDocs.content: </> Ecosystem:AddStateFeature( State: string,-- The States ID Feature: FunctionState-- The function binded to the StateID ) → () Adds a new state with the given ID and binds the function to the ID Reserved key The string Stop is reserved if you want to end the running thread. It is equivalent to calling CurrentSpecies:Stop() Returns Every state function MUST return a valid state ID and a single string. Note: this does NOT mean you can only return 1 state per feature function you may have conditional statements that return different states to execute next. Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") -- State Jump NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Jump10Times", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string local Count = 0 local CurrentObjectHumanoid:Humanoid = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.Humanoid while task.wait(0.1) do if not CurrentObjectHumanoid.Jump then CurrentObjectHumanoid.Jump = true Count += 1 end if Count >= 10 then -- Returns the next state to run return "Wander" end end end) -- State Walk NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Wander", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string local CurrentObjectHumanoid:Humanoid = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.Humanoid local HRP:BasePart = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.HumanoidRootPart local Count = 10 while task.wait(1) do if Count >= 10 then -- Ends the running thread return "Stop" -- reserved end -- Move to a random position with a 50 stud box every second CurrentObjectHumanoid:MoveTo(math.random(-50,50) + HRP.position.X, 0, math.random(-50, 50) + HRP.position.Z) Count += 1 end end)

searchDocs.title: AddCustomProperty​
searchDocs.content: </> Ecosystem:AddCustomProperty( PropertyIndex: string,-- The index of the local property InitialValue: any-- The initial value ) → () Adds a new Local property to the template with a initial value if passed Local All properties created in the FunctionProperties are local and are independent among every species created. They can only be edited at runtime by the Species class Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") -- New local property with a initial value of 20 NPCController:AddCustomProperty("LocalProperty", 20) -- New local property with a initial value of nil NPCController:AddCustomProperty("LocalProperty2")

searchDocs.title: SetFunctionEnviorment​
searchDocs.content: </> Ecosystem:SetFunctionEnviorment( PropertyIndex: string,-- The index of the Global property InitialValue: any-- The initial value ) → () Adds a new Global property to the template with a initial value if passed Global All properties created in the FunctionEnviorment are global and are shared among every species created. They can only be edited at runtime by the Ecosystem class Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") -- New global property with a initial value of 20 NPCController:SetFunctionEnviorment("GlobalProperty", 20) -- New global property with a initial value of nil NPCController:SetFunctionEnviorment("GlobalProperty2")

searchDocs.title: Freeze​
searchDocs.content: </> Ecosystem:Freeze() → () "Freezes" the Ecosystem template allowing Species objects to be created from it Frozen You must freeze a Ecosystem object to create a Species object binded to it. Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("WanderNPC") local Specie1 = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("WanderNPC") -- Error: Ecosystem must be Frozen print(NPCController.Frozen) -- false -- Allows species to be created from 'WanderNPC' class NPCController:Freeze() print(NPCController.Frozen) -- true local Specie1 = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("WanderNPC") -- Returns: Species object from Ecosystem

searchDocs.title: UpdateFunctionEnviorment​
searchDocs.content: </> Ecosystem:UpdateFunctionEnviorment( EnvVar: string,-- The index of the Global property Data: any-- The new data ) → () Updates the global Ecosystem dictionary for all binded and future Species objects Signal Running this function will cause the EnviormentUpdate signal to broadcast Global UpdateFunctionEnviorment will update the GLOBAL dictionary ie: all species will receive the new data instantly Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("WanderNPC") NPCController:SetFunctionEnviorment("MovementSpeed", 16) -- Default value of 16 -- State Walk NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Wander", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string local CurrentObjectHumanoid:Humanoid = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.Humanoid local HRP:BasePart = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.HumanoidRootPart while task.wait(1) do -- Updates the walkspeed to the global attribute value CurrentObjectHumanoid.WalkSpeed = CurrentSpecies:GetEnviorment("MovementSpeed") -- Move to a random position with a 50 stud box every second CurrentObjectHumanoid:MoveTo(math.random(-50,50) + HRP.position.X, 0, math.random(-50, 50) + HRP.position.Z) end end) local Species = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("WanderNPC", NPCModel) -- Starts the runtime loop Species:Start("Wander") while task.wait(2) do -- Updates the enviorment attribute to a random number every 2 seconds NPCController:UpdateFunctionEnviorment("MovementSpeed", math.random(10,40)) end

searchDocs.title: GetEnviorment​
searchDocs.content: </> Ecosystem:GetEnviorment( EnvVar: string?-- The index of the enviorment variable ) → any Returns the value of a enviorment variable with the given index Read-Only The Ecosystem object IS able to write to EnviormentVariables, through UpdateFunctionEnviorment. Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("JumpingNPC") NPCController:SetFunctionEnviorment("GlobalVariable", 10) -- Initial value of 10 print(NPCController:GetEnviorment("GlobalVariable")) -- 10 print(NPCController:GetEnviorment("GlobalVariable2")) -- nil print(NPCController:GetEnviorment()) -- The dictionary of enviorment properties

searchDocs.title: ForceState​
searchDocs.content: </> Ecosystem:ForceState( StateID: string-- A valid stateID ) → () Forces all Species objects binded to the Ecosystem to instantly switch to the next State Example: local NPCController = Ecosystem.NewEcosystem("WanderNPC") -- State Walk NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Wander", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string local CurrentObjectHumanoid:Humanoid = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.Humanoid local HRP:BasePart = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.HumanoidRootPart CurrentObjectHumanoid.WalkSpeed = 16 while task.wait(1) do -- Move to a random position with a 50 stud box every second CurrentObjectHumanoid:MoveTo(math.random(-50,50) + HRP.position.X, 0, math.random(-50, 50) + HRP.position.Z) end end) -- State Stun NPCController:AddStateAndFeature("Stun", function(CurrentSpecies: Ecosystem.Species, StateFrom: string): string local CurrentObjectHumanoid:Humanoid = CurrentSpecies.ObjectBinded.Humanoid CurrentObjectHumanoid.WalkSpeed = 0 task.wait(1) return "Wander" end) -- Creates 5 independent Specie objects binded to the WanderNPC Ecosystem controller for i = 1,5 do local C = NPCModel:Clone() local Species = Ecosystem.NewSpecies("WanderNPC", C) C.parent = workspace Species:Start("Wander") end -- Starts the runtime loop Species:Start("Wander") while task.wait(5) do -- Forces the stun state on all Species for 1 second NPCController:ForceState("Stun") end
